authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis Laws and Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California cannabis regulator source for the governing licensing, inventory, transfer, testing, recall, and recordkeeping baseline used by commercial cannabis operators.

authoritative_sources.title: CannaConnect Compliance Hub
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California licensee workflow hub that centralizes track-and-trace, deliveries, shipments, designated responsibilities, and inspection-facing compliance actions.

authoritative_sources.title: Testing Laboratories
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official regulator workflow and evidence reference for sample handling, certificate-of-analysis upload timing, package linkage, and failed-testing evidence that directly affects release and hold decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Testing, quarantine, and release evidence validation

authoritative_sources.title: METRC
authoritative_sources.publisher: Colorado Department of Revenue, Marijuana Enforcement Division
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Colorado MED source identifying the state traceability platform, training path, system notices, and compliance bulletins that govern inventory and manifest workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis Tracking System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Oregon OLCC workflow reference for CTS access, manifests, transfers, inventory actions, and system responsibilities tied directly to seed-to-sale operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transfer, manifest, and inventory workflow handling

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis Central Reporting System
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington LCB source defining CCRS as the system of record for cannabis inventory, sales, transfers, lab results, and corrections workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reporting, correction, and traceability exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1304 - Records and Reports of Registrants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal controlled-substance recordkeeping standard defining inventories, records, retention, and biennial inventory expectations for DEA registrants.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR 1301.74 - Other Security Controls for Practitioners; Narcotic Treatment Programs; and Other Registrants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal diversion-control rule defining suspicious-order monitoring and reporting duties that this lane must escalate rather than decide autonomously.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction, license, and system-of-record intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify regulator, license type, facility, product class, and governing traceability platform before any compliance conclusion is produced.

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and traceability-control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the relevant seed-to-sale obligations to package lineage, manifest, testing, hold, destruction, inventory, and retention controls for the exact jurisdiction and operating context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and package-lineage review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether tags, batches, packages, manifests, receipts, COAs, quarantine status, recall links, and discrepancy records exist in the correct systems of record with traceable ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and inspection preparation
workflow_stages.description: Convert gaps, public-policy conflicts, failed testing, off-system practices, and missing approvals into remediation packets, inspection-ready summaries, and follow-up owners with explicit blocker logic.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary, signoff, and diversion-sensitive escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, product-release approval, suspicious-order decisions, and formal signoff while routing the issue to the correct authorized owner with preserved evidence context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: License applications, renewals, premises approvals, designated responsible party records, and current license-condition notices
Plant tags, immature batch, harvest batch, production batch, package IDs, inventory adjustments, and destruction witness records
Transfer manifests, receiving confirmations, destination details, vehicle or driver custody logs, and delivery receipts
Sample chain-of-custody records, certificates of analysis, failed-batch holds, quarantine status records, recall notices, and release approvals
State traceability audit history, correction logs, discrepancy investigations, CAPA packets, and inspection or enforcement correspondence
ERP or POS inventory snapshots and reconciliation workpapers tied back to the authoritative traceability platform
If applicable, DEA inventory, biennial inventory, suspicious-order monitoring workpapers, and federally regulated controlled-substance record sets
systems_of_record: State cannabis traceability platform such as Metrc, CCRS, CTS, BioTrack, or state-equivalent database
State licensing portal or regulator filing repository
ERP, manufacturing, or retail POS inventory system
Laboratory information management system or certificate-of-analysis repository
Document management, GRC, or quality evidence repository
Transport, dispatch, or chain-of-custody log system
If applicable, DEA registrant inventory and diversion-monitoring system

allowed: Map seed-to-sale obligations to named controls, evidence artifacts, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, inspection-prep, and remediation summaries for cannabis traceability workflows.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating legal, regulator, lab, or DEA authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, license type, facility, product class, or traceability-platform context.
Conflicting regulator guidance, tenant SOP, public-health hold status, or controlled-substance boundary conditions.
Requests that imply legal interpretation, regulator response, product-release approval, suspicious-order adjudication, or formal signoff.
refuse: Any legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal cannabis or controlled-substance compliance opinion.
Formal signoff on transfer legality, product release, recall closure, destruction approval, or DEA reporting action.
Decisions to alter authoritative state traceability records, approve suspicious-order outcomes, or certify final compliance status.

domain_failure_modes: Treating seed-to-sale compliance as generic GRC work and failing to request jurisdiction, license, package lineage, and traceability-platform context.
Marking readiness complete based on ERP snapshots or spreadsheets when the authoritative state traceability record is missing or inconsistent.
Treating a product as releasable or transferable without COA, hold, quarantine, recall, or manifest evidence linked to package history.
Normalizing off-system operational workarounds for destruction, adjustments, or transfers that bypass required traceability audit history.
Conflating state cannabis traceability support with adjacent DEA-controlled-substance reporting, suspicious-order, or legal-interpretation authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State cannabis traceability platforms and record schemas differ materially by jurisdiction
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack resolves this by requiring runtime retrieval of the tenant's jurisdiction, license scope, and exact traceability platform before any confident output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Federal controlled-substance obligations can define a hard escalation boundary for mixed operators
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack does not guess across that boundary; it routes DEA-regulated reporting, suspicious-order, and legal-interpretation work to adjacent authorized owners.

notes: Sources intentionally combine state cannabis regulators and federal controlled-substance rules so the pack captures both seed-to-sale workflow detail and the adjacent authority boundary where autonomous action must stop.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the remaining jurisdiction differences are handled through explicit runtime retrieval prerequisites and escalation rules.
